name: Old-Fashioned Milk Chocolate Cake W/Frosting
recipeIngredient: 6 ounces Unsweetened chocolate, chopped (1 to 2 pieces)
1 cup Unsalted butter, softened to the consistency of mayonnaise
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together the cake flour, baking soda, baking powder, and salt.
2. In a medium heat-proof bowl set over (not touching) barely simmering water, melt the chocolate in the water, stirring occasionally until perfectly smooth. Remove from heat.
3. In a large mixing bowl, beat the butter on medium speed until creamy. Continue beating while sprinkling in granulated and brown sugars, 1/2 cup at a time. Add vanilla extract and beat until very light.
4. Add eggs singly, beating until thoroughly blended after each addition. Beat until very light and creamy. Blend in the melted chocolate mixture.
5. Alternately add flour and buttermilk by sprinkling over the bowl, then with each addition, mix on the lowest speed just until combined.
6. Divide batter among buttered 9-inch round cake pans with bottoms lined with wax paper. Smooth tops and push batter slightly up against the sides.
7. Bake 2 layers in the middle of the oven rack and 1 layer on the lower oven rack at 350°F, staggering so that the top pans are not directly over the bottom pan.
8. Allow to cool in the pans on racks for 15 minutes. Then turn out onto racks, with the tops up, to cool completely.
9. Up to 6 hours before serving, place the thickest layer, bottoms up, on a platter. Spread with about 2/3 cup frosting. Repeat with the second layer. Place the last layer with the top side up. Frost the top and sides, making decorative sweeps in the frosting with a knife.
10. Keep cool. Do not refrigerate. Just before serving, garnish the leaves around the bottom of the platter.
